differences.title: 1. APPROACH
differences.leftDesc: Build UI by composing small, single-purpose utility classes directly in your markup.
differences.rightDesc: Write actual CSS within JavaScript components, scoping styles automatically.

differences.title: 2. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Easy to grasp utility names and apply them rapidly.
differences.rightDesc: Requires understanding of JavaScript, props, and CSS-in-JS concepts.

differences.title: 3. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Generates minimal CSS, leading to faster initial page loads.
differences.rightDesc: Can introduce a small runtime overhead for style processing.

differences.title: 4. LOCAL DEVELOPMENT
differences.leftDesc: Style directly in HTML/JSX with predefined classes.
differences.rightDesc: Requires setting up components to manage styles.

differences.title: 5. THEMING
differences.leftDesc: Highly customizable through a central `tailwind.config.js` file.
differences.rightDesc: Dynamic theming using JavaScript variables and props.

differences.title: 6. REUSABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Reuse styles by creating custom reusable classes (`@apply`).
differences.rightDesc: Styles are inherently tied to the component, promoting reusability through composition.

differences.title: 7. DESIGN SYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Provides a robust design system foundation to build upon.
differences.rightDesc: Styles are built as part of UI components.
